title: ranunculus sardous subsp. cordigerus
intro: Ranunculus sardous subsp. cordigerus, commonly known as the poisonous buttercup, is a plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ranunculus sardous subsp. cordigerus include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, although its use should be cautious due to its toxicity. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made through infusions or decoctions, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
